content: The College of Health Sciences (CHS), is a professional institution dedicated to advancing various specializations across medical fields. The CHS comprises four schools and one teaching hospital. The four schools are the School of Medicine (SoM), the School of Pharmacy (SoP), the School of Public Health (SPH) and the School of Allied Health Sciences (SAHS). The SAHS offers professional training in nursing, midwifery and medical laboratory technology. The rationale behind the establishment of the CHS is to encourage cross-breeding across various disciplinary paradigms; standardize curricula across disciplines; set standards and quality checks for the teaching-learning process and create opportunities to address the health needs of the community in an integrated manner as a health team.

content: The College of Veterinary Medicine and Agriculture at Addis Ababa University is committed to advancing animal health, agricultural development, and community well-being through excellence in education, research, and veterinary care. The seed for the current College of Veterinary Medicine and Agriculture of the Addis Ababa University was planted in 1963 with the establishment of the School of Animal Health Assistants to train manpower under the Diploma program. The College is currently accomplishing three core missions. These include teaching, problem-solving research and community services. Most of the graduates of the college have joined different positions in Universities, Research Centers, Regional laboratories, Ministry of Agriculture, NGOs, private sectors in the country and abroad.. One of the highest merits of the College is its enduring public services. Though not the only, the major service delivering ones are the Veterinary Teaching Hospital, the Donkey Sanctuary and SPANA.
courses: Bachelor of Degree in Veterinary Science (BVSc)

PhD in Food Security and Development

content: The College of Natural Sciences at AAU fosters a thriving academic environment that promotes excellence in scientific education and research. The College of Natural Sciences (CNS) is one of the flagship institutions of Addis Ababa University, committed to advancing scientific understanding and innovation. The college offers a range of undergraduate and graduate programs designed to equip students with a comprehensive understanding of the natural world, preparing them for careers in research, academia, and industry. Our research facilities are some of the best in the country, fostering collaborative partnerships with both local and international institutions. CNS is also dedicated to promoting interdisciplinary research, ensuring that students are exposed to the interconnectedness of natural sciences in solving real-world problems.

content: The College of Social Sciences, Arts, and Humanities (CSSAH) is Ethiopia’s most diverse academic hub for studying society, culture, and human creativity. Established in 2025 through the merger of the former College of Social Sciences, the College of Development Studies, the College of Performing and Visual Arts, and specialized centers in human rights, federalism, and peace studies, as well as the School of Journalism, CSSAH unites disciplines with deep historical roots and forward-looking visions. The College brings together over a dozen schools, departments, and centers covering fields as wide- ranging as social work, sociology, history, political science, theatre arts, fine arts, music, geography, human rights, development studies, studies of peace and human rights, federalism, and philosophy. This rich academic landscape supports rigorous scholarship, creative practice, and policy engagement that address Ethiopia’s social, cultural, economic, and developmental challenges. With world-class faculty, modern teaching and research facilities, and a vibrant student community, CSSAH is a leading center for undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral training. Our research and engagement activities connect local knowledge with global scholarship, forging partnerships that generate new ideas, influence policy, and strengthen communities. The College catalyzes social transformation and cultural enrichment in Ethiopia and beyond, guided by Addis Ababa University’s strategic vision for 2028 to excel in teaching, research, and public service.
